title: populus pubescens f. mulleriana
intro: Populus pubescens f. mulleriana, also known as Bigtooth Aspen, is a subspecies of the Quaking Aspen tree that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Bigtooth Aspen include reducing fever, relieving arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
